qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in LiveQuery when regexTimeout is disabled
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a ReDoS vulnerability in LiveQuery with a different root cause (regex timeout configuration bypass), different affected components (LiveQuery WebSocket endpoints vs password reset/email verification endpoints), different attack vector (malicious regex via $regex operator vs MongoDB query operators via token field), and different vulnerability class (ReDoS vs NoSQL injection). The advisory describes NoSQL injection via token type validation missing in password reset and email verification endpoints, which is unrelated to the regex evaluation path in LiveQuery.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a NoSQL injection vulnerability in Parse Server's password reset and email verification endpoints where the token field lacks type validation, allowing MongoDB query operator injection to extract tokens. The only qualifying finding describes a completely different ReDoS vulnerability in LiveQuery where disabled regexTimeout allows catastrophic backtracking. These are distinct vulnerabilities with different root causes, affected components, attack vectors, and impact.
evidence: Advisory vulnerability: NoSQL injection via token type in password reset/email verification endpoints (CWE-943)
Finding vulnerability: ReDoS in LiveQuery when regexTimeout is disabled
Different root causes: Token type validation missing vs regex timeout protection bypass
Different affected components: Password reset/email verification endpoints vs LiveQuery WebSocket endpoints
Different attack vectors: MongoDB query operators via token field vs malicious regex via $regex operator
Different impact: Token extraction vs denial of service through event loop blocking
